text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Clean code is easy to read and understand, even for someone who did not write it. One important practice is using clear naming conventions. Variable and function names should describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` rather than `calc`. This makes the code self-documenting. Another key idea is to break tasks into small, focused functions. Each function should do one thing and do it well. This improves maintainability because you can update or fix one small part without affecting the whole program. In short, readable and well-structured code saves time and reduces errors.
